summary: B2 wired the B1 fused selected-expert Q4_K MoE FFN megakernel into the GGUF rows==1 decode path (HIPENGINE_GGUF_FUSED_MOE_FFN, default off, transparent fallback, raw-Q4_K only). It improves the raw-path decode +15.1% and reduces the selected-FFN launches 3->1, but it does NOT beat the deployed T16-repack decode baseline (92.936 tok/s) because the raw megakernel does not apply to the T16 path. Kept gated off; the deployable win is the verify/C_B regime, not GGUF single-token AR decode.

commands.fused_off: HIPENGINE_COMPILER_VERSION_FILE=/tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt PYTHONPATH=. python3 scripts/qwen35_readme_sweep.py --engine gguf --model $GGUF_S --quant gguf_q4_k_s --workloads 512/128 --warmup-runs 1 --measured-runs 2 --warmup-decode-tokens 1 --force-bulk-prefill --bulk-prefill-attention-mode bulk --use-wmma-prefill --no-use-gemv-decode --no-graph-replay-decode --json /tmp/b2_raw_fused_off.json

correctness.b1_kernel_gate: fused vs B0 cpu_reference oracle: f32 kl=9.3e-12/top1=1.0/max_rel=6.9e-6; bf16 kl_mean=2.5e-4/top1=1.0 (KL<=0.05/top1>=90% PASS)
correctness.in_situ_note: On the real model layer 0 (selected=[18,25,160,59,33,201,135,3]), the fused moe_down_out matches the unfused chain to ~1 bf16 ULP (max_abs 1.22e-4); the f32 kernel is bit-exact vs the fp32 cpu_reference oracle on real layer-0 weights (max_rel 1.2e-8). The kernel + wiring are correct.

e2e_teacher_forced_kl.interpretation: Large whole-model teacher-forced KL is 40-layer + KV-drift ACCUMULATION of the unavoidable ~1-ULP bf16 reduction-order difference between two correct implementations (fused per-subblock order vs unfused thread-strided+tree-reduce). NOT a kernel bug (moe_down_out matches to 1 ULP in situ; oracle-exact on real weights). Any kernel swap with a different reduction order changes the exact 40-layer E2E token stream; the project gates correctness at the kernel level (KL<=0.05 vs cpu_reference, PASS), not by E2E token identity.

profiler.fused_kernel_trace: gguf_q4_k_selected_ffn_fused_kernel<unsigned short, unsigned short>, VGPR=104 (post B1.x decode hoist), grid=rows blocks

decision.reason: The kernel is CORRECT (oracle-exact on real weights; 1-ULP from the unfused chain in situ; passes KL<=0.05 vs cpu_reference). Not promoted for two reasons: (1) it reads raw Q4_K and does not apply to the deployed T16-repack decode path, so it cannot beat the 92.936 tok/s baseline (only improves the non-default raw path 9.859->11.343); (2) at single-token decode it is occupancy-bound (only top_k=8 blocks) and its different bf16 reduction order shifts the exact 40-layer E2E token stream. Kept gated off.
next_steps: Batched (c>1) GGUF decode: with c tokens x top_k=8 selected rows the megakernel gets c*8 blocks (e.g. c=8 -> 64), lifting it out of the single-token occupancy wall where it currently loses. The most promising GGUF-megakernel target.
Verify regime (PARO): the megakernel's launch-reduction value is greatest in dispatch-bound speculative verify (the C_B economics), not single-token AR decode.
If pursuing the deployed GGUF decode path specifically: a T16-fused variant (composes with decode-repack) - but it shares the single-token occupancy wall, so batched decode is the likelier win.

notes: Architectural validation (B0 oracle, B1 fused kernel, row-invariance, launch 3->1) is complete and committed; this artifact records the B2 deployment-path measurement and verdict.
Kernel is correct in isolation; the E2E divergence is greedy amplification, not a structural bug (first 3 decode tokens match exactly).
